metadata.title: Guideline on Immunogenicity assessment of therapeutic proteins
metadata.note: Replaces 'Guideline on Immunogenicity assessment of biotechnology-derived therapeutic proteins' (EMEA/CHMP/BMWP/14327/2006). Adopted by CHMP 18 May 2017. In effect 01 December 2017. This is the EMA guideline; a separate FDA guidance on immunogenicity of therapeutic proteins also exists (26-immunogenicity-therapeutic-proteins-rules.json).

rules.rule_text: The evaluation of immunogenicity must be based on integrated analysis of immunological, pharmacokinetic, pharmacodynamic, clinical efficacy, and safety data; immunogenicity issues must also be addressed in the Risk Management Plan (RMP).
rules.verbatim_quote: the evaluation of immunogenicity should be based on integrated analysis of immunological, pharmacokinetic, pharmacodynamic, as well as clinical efficacy and safety data. Immunogenicity issues should be further addressed in the Risk Management Plan (RMP).

rules.rule_text: Applicants must demonstrate that ADA assays are applicable for the demonstration of clinical correlations of ADAs; the development of adequate screening and confirmatory assays to measure immune responses against the therapeutic protein is the basis of the evaluation of immunogenicity.
rules.verbatim_quote: The development of adequate screening and confirmatory assays to measure immune responses against a therapeutic protein is the basis of the evaluation of immunogenicity. The Applicants need to demonstrate that the ADA assays are applicable for the demonstration of clinical correlations of ADAs.

rules.rule_text: The marketing authorization application must include a multidisciplinary summary of immunogenicity containing a risk assessment; the extent and type of pre-authorisation immunogenicity studies and post-marketing risk management program must be tailored according to the risk of immunogenicity and the severity of its potential or observed consequences.
rules.verbatim_quote: the guideline contains a list of issues to be considered, a multidisciplinary summary of immunogenicity, including risk assessment that should be included in the marketing authorization application. This summary allows the justification of risk-based approach to immunogenicity which means that the extent and type of pre-authorisation immunogenicity studies and post-marketing risk management program are tailored according to the risk of immunogenicity and the severity of its potential or observed consequences.

rules.rule_text: Animal studies have low predictive value for evaluating immunogenicity of biological medicinal products in humans due to differences between human and animal immune systems; non-clinical in vitro or in vivo studies aiming at predicting immunogenicity in humans are normally not required.
rules.verbatim_quote: the predictivity of animal studies for evaluation of immunogenicity in humans is considered low. Non-clinical in vitro or in vivo studies aiming at predicting immunogenicity in humans are normally not required.
rules.exceptions: In absence of sufficient knowledge, and if theoretical considerations are suggestive of a safety risk, animal immunization studies with the therapeutic protein or the animal homolog may be considered.

rules.rule_text: If the product is indicated for the pediatric population, clinical studies on immunogenicity are usually expected to be carried out in the relevant age group(s); immunogenicity data from one age group cannot necessarily be projected to others.
rules.verbatim_quote: Data on immunogenicity from one age group cannot necessarily be projected to others, since immune response to therapeutic proteins can be affected by patient age. [...] If the product is indicated for the pediatric population, clinical studies are usually expected to be carried out in the relevant age group(s).

rules.rule_text: If clinical trials of a product with a new active substance are performed in combination with immuno-suppressants, a claim for use of the protein drug in monotherapy must be accompanied by adequate clinical data on the immunogenicity profile in the absence of immuno-suppressive agents.
rules.verbatim_quote: If clinical trials of a product with a new active substance are performed in combination with immuno-suppressants, a claim for use of the protein drug in monotherapy must be accompanied by adequate clinical data on the immunogenicity profile in the absence of immuno-suppressive agents.

rules.rule_text: ADAs against novel constructs containing physiological functional molecules (e.g. fusion proteins) must be investigated for cross-reactivity with relevant endogenous proteins, due to the life-threatening risk of autoimmunity (e.g. pure red cell aplasia from anti-erythropoietin antibodies).
rules.verbatim_quote: ADAs against novel constructs (e.g. fusion proteins containing physiological functional molecules), should be investigated for cross-reactivity with relevant endogenous proteins.

rules.rule_text: Applicants must ensure systematic collection of non-acute clinical sequelae following application of the therapeutic protein; delayed hypersensitivity reactions must be clearly distinguished from infusion-related reactions.
rules.verbatim_quote: Delayed hypersensitivity reactions should be clearly distinguished from infusion-related reactions. Applicants should ensure the systematic collection of non-acute clinical sequelae following application of the therapeutic protein.

rules.rule_text: Clinical studies where immunogenicity is evaluated must be supplied with material sufficiently representative of the medicinal product that is going to be placed on the market; it is preferable to rely on purification processes to remove impurities and contaminants rather than to establish a preclinical testing program for their qualification.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that clinical studies where immunogenicity is evaluated are supplied with material sufficiently representative of the medicinal product that is going to be placed on the market.

rules.rule_text: When ADA measurement is not part of the study protocol in animal toxicology studies, blood samples must be taken and stored for future evaluations when warranted to aid in interpretation of the study results; the assays used must be validated.
rules.verbatim_quote: When ADA measurement is not part of the study protocol, blood samples should be taken and stored for future evaluations when warranted to aid in interpretation of the study results. The assays used should be validated.
rules.exceptions: Generally, there is no need for immunogenicity assessment in single dose toxicity studies. However, for a single dose pharmacokinetic study measurement of ADA could be relevant.

rules.rule_text: Both humoral and cellular immune responses must be considered; cellular responses may be relevant when pharmacodynamic or adverse effects are suspected to be mediated by immune cells, when delayed type hypersensitivity occurs, or when a cytotoxic T-cell response is suspected.
rules.verbatim_quote: Both humoral and cellular immune responses should be considered. Cellular responses may be relevant when pharmacodynamic or adverse effects are (suspected to be) mediated by immune cells, e.g. when delayed type hypersensitivity occurs or when a cytotoxic T-cell response is suspected.

rules.rule_text: For biosimilar development, the comparison of anti-drug antibody response to the biosimilar and the reference product in an animal model is not recommended as part of the biosimilar comparability exercise, due to the low predictivity for immunogenicity potential in humans.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the development of similar biological medicinal products (biosimilars), the comparison of the anti-drug antibody response to the biosimilar and the reference product in an animal model is not recommended as part of the biosimilar comparability exercise, due to the low predictivity for the immunogenicity potential in humans.
rules.exceptions: When in rare occasions there would be a need for a toxicology study or when a pharmacokinetic study is performed there still could be a need to measure ADAs to aid in interpretation of the study results.

rules.rule_text: A multi-tiered approach must be employed for immunogenicity assessment: a screening assay for identification of antibody positive samples/patients, a confirmatory assay for confirming the presence of antibodies and determining antibody specificity, followed by functional assays for the assessment of the neutralizing capacity of antibodies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Typically, a multi-tiered approach should be employed. This includes a screening assay for identification of antibody positive samples/patients, a procedure for confirming the presence of antibodies and determining antibody specificity followed by functional assays for the assessment of the neutralizing capacity of antibodies.
rules.exceptions: Any deviation from this concept should be properly justified and discussed with regulatory authorities before submission of the MA application.

rules.rule_text: All key assays (screening, confirmatory, NAb) must be validated for the intended use; all clinical immunogenicity data to be considered for a MAA must have been obtained using validated assays.
rules.verbatim_quote: All key assays (screening, confirmatory, NAb) need to be validated for the intended use. [...] all clinical immunogenicity data to be considered for a MAA should have been obtained using validated assays.

rules.rule_text: The applicant is expected to provide all necessary information and full assay validation data for assessment as part of the marketing authorization (MA) application, even though assays will be refined during product development.
rules.verbatim_quote: Although assays will be refined during product development and assay suitability reassessed as per their use, the applicant is expected to provide all necessary information and full assay validation data for assessment as part of the marketing authorization (MA) application.

rules.rule_text: Screening assays must be sensitive and capable of detecting all clinically relevant antibodies (including IgM and IgG subclasses) induced against the product in all antibody positive patients; a low false positive rate is desirable (preferably 5%) but false negative results are unacceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: Screening assays are the first step in immunogenicity evaluation. They should be sensitive and capable of detecting all clinically relevant antibodies (including IgM and IgG subclasses) induced against the product in all antibody positive patients. A low false positive rate is desirable (preferably 5%) but false negative results are unacceptable.

rules.rule_text: Matrix effects from complement components, mannose binding protein, Fc receptors, soluble target molecules, and rheumatoid factors must be considered and assessed as part of method validation; corrective measures must be implemented and the chosen approach justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Examples include complement components or complement receptors, mannose binding protein, Fc receptors, soluble target molecules, and rheumatoid factors. The influence of such matrix components on assay results should be considered and assessed as part of method validation. To mitigate the potential influence corrective measures should be implemented and the chosen approach should be justified.

rules.rule_text: The Applicant must demonstrate that the tolerance of the assay to the therapeutic exceeds the levels of the therapeutic protein in the samples for ADA testing; if drug interference occurs, the approach taken must be properly justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: In any case, the Applicant has to demonstrate that the tolerance of the assay to the therapeutic exceeds the levels of the therapeutic protein in the samples for ADA testing. [...] If this occurs, the best possible assay should be employed and the approach taken should be properly justified.
rules.exceptions: Due to technical limitations it may not be always possible to develop fully tolerant assays.

rules.rule_text: Antibodies in confirmed positive samples must be examined for titer and specificity for the therapeutic protein; where antibodies are induced against other substances like product-related and process-related components (e.g. host cell proteins), assays against these impurities must also be developed and validated for testing of patient samples.
rules.verbatim_quote: Antibodies present in confirmed positive samples need to be examined for titer and specificity for the therapeutic protein. It has been shown that antibodies can be induced also against other substances, like product-related and process-related components (e.g. host cell proteins). In such cases it is expected that antibody assays against these impurities are also developed and validated for testing of the patient samples.
rules.exceptions: Levels of the impurities should be kept at a minimum to avoid such immune responses.

rules.rule_text: The neutralizing capacity of antibodies present in positive samples must be evaluated as part of the immunogenicity assessment since this often correlates with diminished clinical responses to biological product; deviation from this concept needs a strong justification and regulatory advice is advisable.
rules.verbatim_quote: The neutralizing capacity of antibodies present in positive samples needs to be evaluated as part of the immunogenicity assessment since this often correlates with diminished clinical responses to biological product. Deviation from this concept needs a strong justification. In such cases, it is advisable to seek regulatory advice.

rules.rule_text: Because neutralizing antibodies (NAbs) can trigger clinical effects, specific and sensitive in vitro methods are expected for detection; for antagonists such as monoclonal antibody therapeutics with effector functions for clinical efficacy, cell-based assays are recommended as the mechanism of action cannot be adequately reflected in a non-cell-based CLB assay.
rules.verbatim_quote: Because NAbs can trigger clinical effects, specific and sensitive in vitro methods are expected for detection. [...] For antagonists such as monoclonal antibody therapeutics with effector functions for clinical efficacy, cell-based assays are recommended as the mechanism of action cannot be adequately reflected in a non-cell-based CLB assay.
rules.exceptions: For products that exert their activity only through direct binding to other molecules (e.g., some MAbs), CLB or other alternatives may be suitable, but it must be demonstrated that they reflect neutralizing capacity/potential in an appropriate manner.

rules.rule_text: For both screening and NAb assays, a panel of reference materials containing different amounts of antibodies (low, medium and high quality controls) must be used for characterizing/validating assays; this must include one or more preparations with low antibody content (close to the minimal detection limit) and containing low avidity antibodies if possible.
rules.verbatim_quote: For both screening and NAb assays, a panel of reference materials that contain different amounts of antibodies (low, medium and high quality controls) are useful for characterizing/validating assays as they act as assay performance indicators. This should include one or more preparations with low antibody content (close to the minimal detection limit) and containing low avidity antibodies, if possible.

rules.rule_text: The identification and/or development of appropriate positive and negative controls is crucial and essential for assay validation; for all controls the characterization data showing their properties and functionality for the intended use must be provided as part of the MAA submission.
rules.verbatim_quote: The identification and/or development of appropriate positive and negative controls is crucial as they are essential for assay validation. [...] For all controls the characterization data showing their properties and functionality for the intended use should be provided as part of the MAA submission.

rules.rule_text: The assays must be validated using the same matrix as the samples to be analysed; baseline response of samples derived from the patient population must be established separately using pre-treatment samples from patients or drug naive disease patients, since baseline for normal healthy individuals may not be representative.
rules.verbatim_quote: Overall, the assays should be validated using the same matrix as the samples to be analysed. [...] this may not be representative of the baseline response of samples derived from the patient population, which would therefore need to be established separately, using pre-treatment samples from patients or drug naïve disease patients.

rules.rule_text: Patients must be screened for pre-existing (pre-treatment) antibodies before treatment so that post-treatment data can be interpreted correctly in terms of treatment-emergent antibodies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Some individual's/patient's samples may contain pre-existing (pre-treatment) antibodies or possibly other substances which produce significant positive responses in assays, and so screening patients for this is necessary to ensure that post-treatment data can be interpreted correctly in terms of treatment emergent antibodies.

rules.rule_text: Reagents used in assays must be qualified and have acceptance criteria set; they must be stored appropriately (lyophilized or frozen at a suitable temperature) and characterized.
rules.verbatim_quote: Reagents used in assays need to be qualified and acceptance criteria set, at least for those which are most important. They should be stored appropriately (lyophilized or frozen at a suitable temperature) and characterized.

rules.rule_text: Assay validation studies must be conducted to establish that the assays show appropriately linear, concentration-dependent responses to relevant analytes as well as appropriate accuracy, precision, sensitivity, specificity and robustness; data supporting minimal required dilution of samples must be included.
rules.verbatim_quote: Validation studies must be conducted to establish that the assays show appropriately linear, concentration dependent responses to relevant analytes as well as appropriate accuracy, precision, sensitivity, specificity and robustness. Inclusion of data supporting minimal required dilution of samples is important.

rules.rule_text: Assays must be validated to show that matrix effects caused by reagents or substances present in samples or drug interference do not adversely affect the results; this must be investigated for the full range of dilutions of samples which are to be used in assays.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assays must also be validated to show that matrix effects caused by reagents or substances present in samples or drug interference do not adversely affect the results obtained. [...] This needs to be investigated for the full range of dilutions of samples, which are to be used in assays.

rules.rule_text: Clear criteria must be established for deciding how samples will be considered positive or negative, and also how positive results will be confirmed; the approach taken must be justified by data.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is essential to establish clear criteria for deciding how samples will be considered positive or negative, and also how positive results will be confirmed. The approach taken should be justified by data.

rules.rule_text: For biosimilar comparability exercises, immunogenicity testing of the biosimilar and the reference product must be conducted within the same exercise by using the same assay format and sampling schedule; the assays should preferably be capable of detecting antibodies against all epitopes of both biosimilar and reference molecule.
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunogenicity testing of the biosimilar and the reference product should be conducted within the biosimilar comparability exercise by using the same assay format and sampling schedule. The assays should preferably be capable of detecting antibodies against all epitopes of both biosimilar and reference molecule.
rules.exceptions: If separate assays are used, this two-antigen assay approach requires careful validation to exclude any bias due to differences in sensitivity and drug tolerance.

rules.rule_text: Regardless of the approach used for immunogenicity assessment of a biosimilar product versus the reference product, the assays must be cross-validated using both antigens, antibody positive controls and preferably clinical samples to demonstrate similar performance.
rules.verbatim_quote: Regardless of the approach used for immunogenicity assessment of a biosimilar product versus the reference product, it is recommended that the assays are cross-validated using both antigens, antibody positive controls and preferably clinical samples to demonstrate similar performance.

rules.rule_text: For conjugated proteins and fusion proteins, multiple assays may be required to measure immune responses to various moieties; for pegylated proteins, the assessment strategy must comprise a screening assay using the pegylated therapeutic and testing of any positive samples using the whole therapeutic, the non-pegylated protein, and the PEG moiety in a confirmatory assay.
rules.verbatim_quote: the characterization of the specificity of the induced antibodies is challenging and may require multiple assays for measuring immune responses to various moieties. [...] For example, for a pegylated protein, the assessment strategy would comprise a screening assay using the pegylated therapeutic and testing of any positive samples using the whole therapeutic, the non-pegylated protein and the polyethylene glycol (PEG) moiety in a confirmatory assay.

rules.rule_text: Cross-reactivity of the ADAs with relevant endogenous proteins must be investigated if emerging autoimmunity is suspected; normally, the incidence, titre, persistence, and neutralizing capacity of the ADAs are required to be characterized.
rules.verbatim_quote: Normally, the incidence and titre, persistence and neutralizing capacity of the ADAs are required. [...] Cross-reactivity of the ADAs with relevant endogenous proteins should be investigated if emerging autoimmunity is suspected.

rules.rule_text: Testing of immunogenicity must be included in all pivotal clinical pharmacokinetic, pharmacodynamics, safety, and efficacy trials of a biological medicinal product; assessment of immunogenicity must be included in the planning of the pivotal clinical trials, including the synchronization of sampling for ADAs and relevant biomarkers.
rules.verbatim_quote: Testing of immunogenicity should be included in all pivotal clinical pharmacokinetic, pharmacodynamics, safety, and efficacy trials of a biological medicinal product [...] assessment of immunogenicity should be included in the planning of the pivotal clinical trials, including the synchronization of sampling for ADAs and relevant biomarkers, if available, as well as evaluation of efficacy and safety.
rules.exceptions: Special clinical immunogenicity studies are generally not needed.

rules.rule_text: Baseline samples must always be collected; immunogenicity must be systematically tested in patients by scheduled routine repetitive sampling as well as in a symptom-driven manner with additional samples when the occurrence of an unwanted immune response is suspected.
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunogenicity should be systematically tested in patients by scheduled routine repetitive sampling as well as in a symptom-driven manner with additional samples, when the occurrence of an unwanted immune response is suspected. [...] Baseline samples should always be collected.

rules.rule_text: In case of continuous chronic treatment, immunogenicity data for one year of treatment must normally be available pre-authorisation; shorter follow up is possible with a proper justification.
rules.verbatim_quote: In case of continuous chronic treatment, immunogenicity data for one year of treatment should normally be available pre-authorisation but shorter follow up is possible with a proper justification.
rules.exceptions: Shorter follow up possible with proper justification.

rules.rule_text: If the product has different routes of administration, Applicants must justify their approach as regards immunogenicity assessment for each route at the time of Marketing Authorisation Application.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the product has different routes of administration, Applicants should justify their approach as regards immunogenicity assessment for each route at the time of Marketing Authorisation Application.

rules.rule_text: Patients with pre-existing ADAs may exhibit a different efficacy and safety profile and must be analysed as a subgroup when feasible; the analysis plan must define symptom complexes that might be associated with acute or delayed hypersensitivity and autoimmunity as well as with the loss of efficacy.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with pre-existing ADAs may exhibit a different efficacy and safety profile and should be analysed as a subgroup when feasible. The analysis plan should define symptom complexes that might be associated with acute or delayed hypersensitivity and autoimmunity as well as with the loss of efficacy.

rules.rule_text: Head-to-head comparative immunogenicity studies are required in the development of biosimilar products; immunogenicity evaluation as part of a clinical trial for a comparability exercise in the context of a manufacturing change should preferably involve head-to-head study of pre- and post-change product.
rules.verbatim_quote: Head-to-head comparative immunogenicity studies are required in the development of biosimilar products [...] Immunogenicity evaluation as part of a clinical trial for a comparability exercise in the context of a manufacturing change should preferably involve head-to-head study of pre- and post-change product.

rules.rule_text: As part of the application for marketing authorisation, the applicant must present a risk management plan (RMP) in accordance with current EU legislation and pharmacovigilance guidelines; immunogenicity must be considered for the safety specification section of the RMP of therapeutic proteins.
rules.verbatim_quote: As part of the application for marketing authorisation, the applicant should present a risk management plan (RMP) in accordance with current EU legislation and pharmacovigilance guidelines (see EU-GVP Module V). Immunogenicity must be considered for the safety specification section of the RMP of therapeutic proteins.
rules.exceptions: If no particular concern or uncertainty arises from the evaluation, inclusion by default of immunogenicity as a potential risk or as an area of missing information is not required.

rules.rule_text: For changes in the manufacturing process, implications of the change on the immunogenic potential must be addressed in the RMP; the need for additional pharmacovigilance activities must be evaluated and assessed.
rules.verbatim_quote: For changes in the manufacturing process, implications of this change on the immunogenic potential might have to be addressed in the RMP. [...] Within the pharmacovigilance plan of the RMP, the need for additional pharmacovigilance activities should be evaluated and assessed.

rules.rule_text: Traceability of product and batch number suspected to cause adverse reactions is important for therapeutic proteins; appropriate measures to improve traceability, in particular collection of brand name and batch number, must be taken.
rules.verbatim_quote: Identification of the product and batch number suspected to cause an adverse reaction, traceability, is important for therapeutic proteins. This is especially important for adverse events related to immunogenicity [...] Appropriate measures to improve traceability, in particular collection of brand name and batch number, should be taken (see EU-GVP P.II).
